construct_verdict_reason: Items p13-p16 directly measure normative beliefs about ideal family structure and ideology, but p67-p68 measure descriptive observations about family change rather than normative beliefs about what should be ideal.

items.reason: Directly asks respondents to identify their conception of an ideal family structure, core to measuring family normativity.
Asks about barriers to achieving the ideal family type, which reflects normative ideology about what prevents family ideals.
Asks what actions could enable achievement of ideal family, reflecting normative beliefs about solutions and values.
Directly measures acceptance or rejection of non-traditional family arrangements, capturing traditionalism versus pluralism in family ideology.
Measures descriptive observations about actual family changes across generations rather than normative beliefs about what constitutes an ideal family.
Measures predictions about future family trends rather than normative beliefs about what family structure should ideally be.
